key_insight: The paper shows that physics-augmented neural networks can be deployed as practical hyperelastic constitutive models inside explicit finite element solvers without needing a specialized ML-enabled code.
engineering_relevance: This enables data-driven material laws for impact and large-strain simulations in Simcenter Radioss and OpenRadioss while preserving the robustness and workflow of established engineering FE tools.
method: Train hyperelastic PANNs in PyTorch with embedded physical constraints.
Export the trained network architecture and weights into standalone Fortran user material routines.
Compare activation functions and optimize evaluation cost, including replacing SoftPlus with SQuarePlus.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports an example impact simulation in which the generated user material reproduces the nonlinear large-strain response characteristic of hyperelastic materials.
watch_item: Deployment depends on careful generation and validation of the user material routine, since network architecture and activation choice directly affect runtime cost and solver performance.
paper_title: Implementation of Hyperelastic Physics-Augmented Neural Networks in the Explicit Finite Element Codes Simcenter Radioss and OpenRadioss with Applications to Impact Events
